title: When Noticing a
subtitle: Pause Before Calling It New
description:
When noticing a "new" trend:
- Research its history: Check if this has actually been around for a while.
- Ask others: See if someone older or more experienced recognizes it as old news.
- Separate novelty from attention: Just because you noticed it recently doesn’t mean it’s new.

Example: A "new" slang word? Look it up in older dictionaries or forums to see its origins.

howTo.title: Define the claim precisely
howTo.summary: Identify the trend you noticed and phrase it as a clear, testable statement. This helps focus your research and reduces scatter in your searches.

howTo.title: Conduct archival research
howTo.summary: Use news archives, community forums, and digitized books to check the history of the trend. Look for evidence that contradicts the novelty claim.

howTo.title: Consult an experienced source
howTo.summary: Reach out to someone older or more knowledgeable about the trend. Ask if they recognize it and for any pointers on its history.

howTo.title: Differentiate novelty from attention
howTo.summary: Analyze whether the trend is genuinely new or just receiving attention. Check for temporal and geographic spread to assess its significance.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your findings and decisions in the Brali LifeOS app to track your research and improve future trend assessments. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/trend-novelty-checker/.
